method_atom: a rigorous, convergent renormalization-group expansion for a many-body system of interacting units whose low-energy excitations vanish linearly around isolated points (a linearly-dispersing, Dirac-like spectrum), proving analyticity of the perturbation series for thermodynamic and correlation functions and showing that a weak short-range interaction only renormalizes effective parameters (a propagation velocity and a field-normalization constant) without shifting the infrared fixed point, thereby excluding symmetry-breaking or ordering instabilities; conserved-current (Ward) identities further constrain the correlation functions up to negligible corrections
object_atom: electrons in a 2D Hubbard model on the honeycomb lattice at half filling with weak screened Coulomb interactions, modeling a single-layer graphene sheet
brief_reason: the constructive RG proof that weak interactions merely renormalize velocity and wave function around a linear-dispersion fixed point is the transferable machinery, while the half-filled honeycomb-lattice electron system standing in for graphene is the condensed-matter-specific object.
